title: Lithodora zahnii
intro: Lithodora zahnii, also known as Lithodora, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Lithodora include alkaloids, such as lithospermine and zahnine, as well as glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Lithodora is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Lithodora zahnii is classified as a species within the genus Lithodora, which is a part of the family Boraginaceae. This genus is a group of flowering plants commonly known as blue lobelias. Lithodora zahnii is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is a low-growing, spreading perennial that produces small, blue flowers. It is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens and landscapes.
